mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment directly reduces household consumption capacity and corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when UNRATE increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate increase above the 0.25% threshold signals the end of the zero-interest-rate policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in >65% of instances when FEDFUNDS crosses 0.25%, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening corporate output capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Sub-1.0% quarterly GDP growth signals stagnating aggregate demand and weak corporate revenue trajectories, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth remains below 1.0%, or the threshold signal shows no predictive alpha (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll employment growth and unemployment rate declines confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal produces negative 20-day SPX returns in >50% of triggered months, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly signals rising nominal earnings expectations and inflation hedging demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing activity and reduced corporate output, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in >60% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.015 across rolling 3-year windows.
